Output bundle format unchanged so `map_ariregister()` in `bods/mapper.py` is unmodified. `requires_api_key=False`, `live_available=True` always. Live-tested against Nordic Foods 1 OÜ (1 officer, 3 corporate shareholders, 2 BOs). **(2) BOVS risk signal overlays** on ownership diagrams — coloured pill badges at 315° NW circumference per BOVS Metadata Overlays spec: single signal → labelled pill (e.g. "S" for Sanctioned, "P" for PEP) in matching colour; multiple signals → "N ⚠" stack badge in worst-severity colour with shadow-circle depth indicator. Signal colours match `RiskChip.tsx` palette: rose (`SANCTIONED`, `RELATED_SANCTIONED`, `FATF_BLACK_LIST`), violet (`PEP`, `RELATED_PEP`), orange (`FATF_GREY_LIST`, `NON_EU_JURISDICTION`), etc. Backend: `statement_id` added to `SANCTIONED`/`PEP` signal evidence so the frontend resolves which BODS node to overlay. `buildSignalMap()` reads evidence fields from all signal types (`evidence.statement_id`, `evidence.subject_statement_id`, `evidence.matches[].statement_id`, `evidence.jurisdictions[].statement_id`, `evidence.longest_path[]`). Legend strip in diagram toolbar extended with Sanction/PEP/Jurisdiction swatches when signals are present. | | 46 | Sudski registar (Croatia) adapter — entity data from the Croatian Court Register via the public `sudreg_javni` v3 JSON API (`https://sudreg-data.gov.hr/api/javni`); `hr_mbs` derived from GLEIF RA code `RA000156` (Croatian Court Registry), taken from the `registeredAs` field (zero-padded `potpuni_mbs`, e.g. `080000604`). Officers/BO not exposed by the public API → entity statements only. Not-found/invalid identifiers return HTTP 400 with an `error_code` body → treated as stub. Wired into both `/lookup` and `/lookup-stream`. Requires `SUDREG_CLIENT_ID` / `SUDREG_CLIENT_SECRET` (free from sudreg-data.gov.hr; both end in literal `..`). `tests/test_sudreg_croatia.py` adds 25 tests. | | 47 | Cyprus DRCOR adapter (committed, **inactive** — bulk-only) — company + officer data from the Department of Registrar of Companies and Intellectual Property via three monthly data.gov.cy CSVs (organisations, registered office, officials; no shareholders) under CC BY 4.0; `cy_he` derived from GLEIF RA code `RA000161` (DRCOR Companies Section), taken from the `registeredAs` field (Greek-script `ΗΕ` number normalised to digits). data.gov.cy exposes no query API — `/api/1/datastore/query` returns HTTP 404 and the ≈126 MB officials CSV is not datastore-imported — so the adapter follows the ACRA/BCE local-SQLite pattern: the CSVs are built into a local DB via `scripts/extract_cyprus.py` and queried by registration number; maps a company `entityStatement` + per-official person/entity statement + `seniorManagingOfficial` `relationshipStatement`. **Not in `REGISTRY` and not wired into the `/lookup` or `/lookup-stream` dispatch — never shown on `/sources`**; listed under [Inactive / bulk-only adapters](sources.md#inactive--bulk-only-adapters) alongside ACRA (Singapore) and BCE (Belgium). Activate via `CYPRUS_DRCOR_DB_FILE`. `tests/test_cyprus_drcor.py` adds 14 tests. | | 48 | Australian Business Register (ABN Lookup) adapter — live, free adapter over the ABR ABN Lookup JSON web services (`abr.business.gov.au/json`), GUID auth via `ABN_GUID`. Unwraps JSONP callback wrapper client-side; routes by digit length (11 → `AbnDetails` endpoint, 9 → `AcnDetails` endpoint). GLEIF bridge uses two RA codes: `RA000014` (ASIC) → `au_acn` (9-digit ACN, normalised from GLEIF's spaced format) and `RA000013` (ABR/ATO) → `au_abn` (11-digit ABN). Entity-level firmographic data only — ABN, ACN, entity name and type, ABN/GST status, registered state and postcode, and business (trading) names — mapped to a single BODS entity statement with `AU-ABN` + `AU-ACN` identifiers; trading names emitted as `alternateNames`; state + postcode as partial registered address; `entityType.subtype` populated from the `EntityTypeName` field (e.g. "Commonwealth Government Entity"). Cancelled ABNs (`AbnStatus != "Active"`) annotated with `dissolutionDate`. Wired into both `/lookup` and `/lookup-stream`. License: CC BY 3.0 AU (Australian Taxation Office). Requires a free `ABN_GUID` from [abr.business.gov.au/Documentation/WebServiceRegistration](https://abr.business.gov.au/Documentation/WebServiceRegistration). `tests/test_abr_australia.py` adds 14 tests. | | 49 | Graph-native demo — curated BODS subgraph pipeline, Neo4j CSV export, slide viewer, licensing | | 50 | National ID search — three-tab search panel (Company name \| National ID \| Paste an LEI). Users enter a local registration number and select a country; `searchByNationalId()` queries all three GLEIF registration-ID filter fields in parallel (`filter[entity.registeredAs]`, `filter[registration.validatedAs]`, `filter[registration.otherValidationAuthorities.validatedAs]`), each scoped to the registration authority RA code to prevent false matches from coincidental ID collisions. Covers all 17 countries with active adapters: AT, BE, CA, HR, DK, EE, FR, IE, LV, LT, NL, NO, PL, SG, SK, SE, GB. Single result → auto-navigate to `/lookup-stream`; multiple → picker; zero → amber "not found" notice with "try by name" fallback. Per-country format validation is advisory and non-blocking: amber border + inline hint fires after `onBlur` only (`nationalIdTouched` state), never preventing submission since GLEIF may store IDs in a normalised form. Two new files: `frontend/src/lib/raCodes.ts` (RA codes, labels, placeholders, format regexes, `COUNTRY_OPTIONS`, `validateNationalId()`) and `frontend/src/lib/gleifNationalId.ts` (`GleifSearchResult`, `searchByNationalId()`). Pure frontend change — no backend routes added or modified. Commit `9f4e79b`. | | 51 | Backend & frontend hardening — five architecture improvements from a Claude Fable 5 review of a codebase built to this point with Sonnet and Opus. **(1) One lookup pipeline** (`7282b7a`): `/lookup` and `/lookup-stream` were two hand-synchronised copies (~600–700 duplicated lines) — the source of the Corporations Canada regression `603c086`. A single async generator `_lookup_pipeline()` now resolves the GLEIF anchor, builds derived identifiers, dispatches adapters, streams results, reconciles, deepens and merges risk; the stream serialises its events, the sync endpoint collects them (2,403 → 1,159 lines). Flushed out three latent bugs: duplicate live-mode fetches on the sync path, double-appended deepen pairs on the stream, and wrong Norway/Sweden RA codes in the notes (now `RA000472` brreg, `RA000544` bolagsverket, verified live). **(2) Self-describing adapters** (`86a5a3e`): each register adapter declares its lookup wiring as class attributes (`lookup_derivers`, `lookup_dispatch_keys`, `lookup_pass_legal_name`); `routers/lookup.py` builds its dispatch tables from the registry at import and raises if an adapter declares lookup keys without a `_bh_()` hit builder. The `_MAPPERS` dict and the 60-line import wall are gone — mappers resolve by the `map_` convention. Hand-maintained expected-source lists in `test_sources.py`/`test_app.py` replaced by filesystem discovery (`_DELIBERATELY_UNREGISTERED` allowlist for bulk-only adapters). **(3) Shareable & survivable lookups** (`e3058ce`): in-memory replay cache for completed runs (15-min TTL, keyed `LEI:deepen_top`) so refresh/shared-URL/reconnect is near-instant; `?refresh=true` bypasses; new `GET /lookup-source?lei=&source_id=` re-runs one source. Frontend: lookups addressable via `?lei=` (pushState + popstate), a stream drop after the anchor resolves keeps partial results behind a "Resume lookup" banner, and failed source cards get a "Retry source" button. **(4) Cold-start & tail-latency** (`ca4a6c1`): the FastAPI lifespan now warms the GEM/GLEIF/GEOT indexes in a background thread (`climatetrace.warm_caches()`, non-fatal on failure) and the lazy index builds run via `asyncio.to_thread` so they never block the event loop; every adapter declares a `lookup_timeout_s` wall-clock budget (default 30 s; cvr_denmark 90 s, openaleph 60 s) enforced with `asyncio.wait_for`, emitting `source_error` with `error_type: "timeout"`. **(5) Code-split bundle** (`c76b89c`): `BODSGraph` (Cytoscape + cytoscape-dagre) lazy-loaded via `React.lazy`/`Suspense` since it only renders on "Visualise" — main bundle 777 kB → 271 kB (244 → 80 kB gzip), ~67% cut to first paint; unused `zustand` dependency removed. New `tests/test_lookup_pipeline.py` pins sync/stream parity, the wiring invariants, replay-cache semantics and per-source budgets. | | 52 | GEM Global Energy Ownership Tracker (GEOT) project-level ESG data — expands the Climate TRACE / GEM source beyond emissions to ownership reach. **(1) climatetrace adapter fixes** (`3373c31`): `_parse_parents()` now strips the ownership-share suffix GEM embeds in both parent columns (`E100000000817 [55.0%]` — ~12,000 of ~13,600 populated rows previously left the bracket in the parsed entity ID) and surfaces the share as a float, which the BODS mapper emits as `share.exact`; data download switches GCS-bucket-first (the GitHub `ownership.zip` lagged the Climate TRACE bucket — Feb vs May 2026 — with the zip kept as offline/test fallback); a cycle-safe ownership summary walks the entity→entity graph to count direct vs group-wide Climate TRACE assets, subsidiaries and a sector breakdown. **(2) Per-LEI project portfolio** (`2f1e9d2`): because the GEOT xlsx sits behind a reCAPTCHA download form, `scripts/build_geot_projects.py` condenses each release's nine per-tracker ownership-closure sheets (coal/gas/bioenergy plants, coal/iron mines, gas & oil/NGL pipelines, steel, cement) into the committed artifact `backend/opencheck/data/geot_projects.json.gz` (99 KB, 10,734 entities from 246k closure rows). Counting recipe handles the per-sheet column quirks (coal's `Owner GEM Entity ID` vs others' `Parent GEM Entity ID`, iron's `Operating status`, the per-tracker asset-ID columns), dedupes exact-duplicate and "timepoint" rows, sums distinct paths to a unit capped at 100 %, takes the max share across a project's units (≥50 % = controlled), and assigns each project its best status (operating > development > mothballed > retired > cancelled). The ESG card is redesigned into a two-stat header — emissions (linked to Climate TRACE) alongside "N live projects · X operating · Y ≥50 % controlled" (linked to the GEM Ownership Tracker) — above a per-tracker table with live/operating/≥50 % columns and proportional bars, a mothballed/retired/cancelled footnote, a "group reach" line, and parent pills that now show ownership shares. `ATTRIBUTIONS.md` cites the GEOT release per GEM's recommended wording. Rerunning for a new release is one command (`--release "Jul 2026"`). | | 53 | AI summaries — a grounded, source-cited narrative of an entity for a customer-due-diligence / financial-crime audience; every statement is backed by OpenCheck's own data, enforced mechanically rather than by prompt wording alone. **(1) Offline core** (`4fa5296`): new `opencheck/narrative/` package — `build_evidence_packet()` distils a `/lookup` (or `/report`) result into an `EvidencePacket` of atomic, already-evidenced **facts** (each with source name, adapter `source_id`, BODS statement ids and a confidence derived from source authority — `officialRegister` → high, else medium), structured **risk items**, **sources consulted** (with licence) and **gaps** (CDD-relevant absences); `summarise()` calls Claude (`claude-sonnet-4-6`, structured tool output, temperature 0) for one executive paragraph plus per-claim citations referencing evidence ids (`f`/`r`/`g`); `validate_narrative()` drops any claim citing an unknown or empty id and withholds the paragraph on violation, so "no unprovable information" is guaranteed in code. Absence is treated as evidence — clean entities carry a synthesised "the risk engine ran and found nothing" fact and every gap is itself a citable item, so the model can state "no beneficial owner was disclosed" or "no risks were found" *with* a citation rather than fabricating one. Compliance-analyst tone: risks framed as structural/jurisdictional indicators for review (never determinations of wrongdoing), confidence stated per signal, name-matches and incomplete screening caveated, internal signal codes kept out of the prose.
